source: American Academy of Otolaryngology-Head and Neck Surgery
title: Earwax and Care
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Earwax is normally protective and usually does not need removal unless it causes symptoms or blocks assessment.
Impacted earwax can cause hearing difficulty, fullness, itching, pain, ringing, or dizziness.
Patients should avoid putting objects or candles into the ear canal.
Clinicians may treat cerumen impaction with cerumenolytic drops, irrigation, or manual removal when safe.
